text: Single lung ventilation was a preferred type of anesthesia and the left lateral decubitus position was given with the right arm abducted overhead.
A 10 mm port placed in infrascapular region and two 12 mm ports were placed in the posterior axillary line in the 6th intercostal space and 9th intercostal space under vision.
We could appreciate a multiloculated air-filled cystic lesion in medial basal segment of the right lower lobe.
Few additions were present which required a blunt dissection.
Non-anatomical wedge dissection is being carried out by using an Ethicon 45 mm stapler with the white load.
As we can see that the staples are inserted between the pathological tissue and the normal lung tissue.
They are fired under the vision without holding any major vessels in between.
So staplers are so good in such a way they achieve a proper hemostasis as well as separates the pathological lesion from the normal lung tissue by cutting using the white loans.
Staplers have got a very good advantage is that it seals the air leaks and the small airways so as to prevent further pneumothorax.
As we can see the cystic lung tissue is being separated from the normal lung by using an endostapler 45 mm by using the white load.
It helps in good cutting of the tissues and achieves the hemostasis as well.
The specimen is separated and it is retrieved and sent for pathological examination.
As we can see, the external surface of the specimen is cystic in nature and it is lined by the staplers.
This is a picture of a port closure.
